data.title: Thursday’s Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:20 AM Raphael Bostic Speaks

Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic gives opening remarks before the Banking Outlook Conference: “The Next Horizon in Banking" hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta.

8:30 AM International Trade in Goods (Advance)

This monthly report offers advanced import and export data on the goods components of the monthly trade report.

8:30 AM International Trade in Goods and Services

Updating the goods portion of the advance report and offering initial data on services, this report provides complete information on cross-border trade.

Forecasters expect the trade gap to be up at $55.8 billion for December compared with $56.8 billion in November.

8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time.

Claims are expected to be nearly flat at 225K versus 227K in the previous week.

8:30 AM Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index

The general conditions index from this business outlook survey is a diffusion index of manufacturing conditions within the Philadelphia Federal Reserve district.

The consensus looks for an index at 7.7 in February, suggesting ongoing modest expansion in manufacturing business versus 12.6 in January. 

8:30 AM Michelle Bowman Speaks

Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman gives opening remarks before the Banking Outlook Conference:"The Next Horizon in Banking" hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta.

8:30 AM Retail Inventories (Advance)

Retail inventories measure the monthly dollar value of inventories held by retailers.

8:30 AM Wholesale Inventories (Advance)

Wholesale inventories measure the monthly dollar value of inventories held by merchant wholesalers and are tracked to gauge inventory change in quarterly GDP.

9:00 AM Neel Kashkari Speaks

Minneapolis Federal Reserve Bank President Neel Kashkari gives a keynote before the Midwest Summit: Economic Outlook 2026 hosted by the Fargo Moorhead West Fargo Chamber of Commerce.

10:00 AM Pending Home Sales Index 

The National Association of Realtors developed the pending home sales index as a leading indicator of housing activity.

Pending home sales are seen rebounding by 2.5 percent in January after dropping 9.3 percent in December.

10:00 AM Leading Indicators

The index of leading economic indicators is a composite of 10 forward-looking components, including building permits, new factory orders, and unemployment claims.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country.

10:30 AM Austan Goolsbee Speaks

Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee gives opening remarks before the 2026 Joint Conference on Financial Crises co-hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago.

12:00 PM EIA Petroleum Status Report 

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on petroleum inventories in the U.S., whether produced here or abroad.

1:00 PM 30-Yr TIPS Auction

The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $8 billion of 30-year notes. 

2:30 PM Austan Goolsbee Speaks

Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee gives opening remarks before the 2026 Joint Conference on Financial Crises co-hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago. 

4:30 Fed Balance Sheet 

The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

data.title: Fortuna Silver Mines Non-GAAP EPS of $0.23 in-line, revenue of $270.2M
data.description:
• Fortuna Silver Mines press release (FSM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.23 in-line.
  • Revenue of $270.2M (+38.4% Y/Y).
  • Record quarterly and annual free cash flow1 of $132.3 million and $330.0 million as Fortuna delivers on its operational plan and achieves production guidance.
  • Record free cash flow1 from ongoing operations of $132.3 million; $330.0 million for 2025
  • $147.6 million of net cash from operating activities before changes in working capital or $0.48 per share; $455.4 million for the year or $1.48 per share
  • Liquidity increased to $704.0 million, and the net cash1 position strengthened to $381.5 million, from $58.8 million at the end of 2024, a YoY increase of $322.7 million
  • Quarter-end cash balance of $554.0 million, an increase of $115.7 million QoQ and $322.7 million YoY.
  • Gold equivalent production (“GEO”) of 65,130 ounces; 317,001 GEOs in 2025 meeting annual guidance
  • Consolidated cash cost per GEO1 of $971; $944 for 2025 in line with guidance
  • Consolidated AISC per GEO1 of $2,054 for Q4 2025 and $1,870 for full year 2025. Excluding the impact of rising gold prices on royalties ($60/ounce), gold equivalent ratios ($54/ounce) and the value of the Company’s shares increasing share based compensation expenses ($60/ounce) AISC was $1,696 and within guidance.

data.title: Blue Owl sells loans, raises $1.4B for investor payouts
data.description:
Certain Blue Owl (OWL) BDCs on Wednesday said that they have entered into separate definitive agreements with four North American public pension and insurance investors to opportunistically sell $1.4 billion of direct lending investments at 99.7% of par value as of February 12, 2026.

The transaction provides significant return of capital distribution to OBDC II shareholders, the company said in a statement. It also provides OTIC and OBDC with greater flexibility to deploy capital into "what we believe will be an attractive direct lending environment and modestly increases portfolio diversification."

Pro forma for the transaction, OTIC is expected to have cash, undrawn debt capacity and liquid loans in excess of $1.6 billion as of January 31, 2026.

These sales include debt investment commitments of $600 million from Blue Owl Capital Corporation II, $400 million from Blue Owl Technology Income Corp and $400 million from Blue Owl Capital Corporation (OBDC), representing approximately 34%, 6% and 2% of total investment commitments, including unfunded commitments, at each BDC respectively.

Each investment to be sold represents a partial amount of each Blue Owl BDC's exposure to the respective portfolio company. 

The settlement of the sales is expected to be completed in the first quarter of 2026, Blue Owl (OWL) said.

data.title: Samsung shares hit record high on report of 30% AI chip price increase
data.description:
Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) shares jumped to a fresh record high on Thursday, after local media reported that the firm is negotiating a price for its latest artificial intelligence memory chip that's up to 30% higher than the previous generation.

The chipmaker’s stock rose as much as 5.4% on the Korea Exchange as the market reopened following a three-day holiday. Samsung (SSNLF) is looking to price its HBM4 at around $700 per unit, according to the Chosun Ilbo newspaper.

Samsung (SSNLF) last week said it has begun mass production of its latest high-bandwidth memory chips, HBM4, and has shipped commercial products to customers. The move positions the chipmaker to strengthen its supply to Nvidia (NVDA) as it competes with industry rivals in the high-performance memory market.

Samsung, ‌the world's top memory ⁠chipmaker, has said that its HBM4 delivers a consistent processing speed of 11.7 gigabits-per-second (Gbps), a 22% increase from its ​predecessor, HBM3E.

data.title: Sonic Automotive outlines EchoPark expansion to reach 1M+ units amid strategic marketing investment
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sonic Automotive (SAH) Q4 2025

Management View

  • David Smith, CEO & Chairman, opened by highlighting, "2025 marked the third consecutive year of delivering all-time record customer satisfaction scores for our franchise dealership guests. And EchoPark once again retained the highest guest satisfaction rating among pre-owned vehicle retailers." He shared that reported GAAP EPS was $1.36 per share, with adjusted EPS at $1.52 per share, a 1% increase year-over-year, and consolidated total revenues at $3.9 billion, down 1% year-over-year. Smith emphasized record gross profit and adjusted EBITDA results and noted, "For the full year, reported GAAP EPS was $3.42 per share, and adjusted EPS was $6.60 per share, an 18% increase from 2024. Consolidated total revenues were an all-time annual record of $15.2 billion, up 7% year-over-year."
  • Smith announced that EchoPark segment performance reached record adjusted segment income of $3.6 million and adjusted EBITDA of $8.8 million for the quarter. He stated, "We remain focused on increasing our mix of non-auction sourced inventory to benefit consumer affordability and retail sales volume and GPU."
  • The company plans a disciplined store opening cadence for EchoPark starting late 2026, aiming to reach 90% of U.S. car buyers and sell over 1 million vehicles annually. Smith said, "We expect to begin to invest in this effort during 2026, potentially increasing advertising expense by $10 million to $20 million this year."
  • Smith highlighted a strong liquidity position with $702 million available and $306 million in cash and floor plan deposits. The Board approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.38 per share.
  • Heath R. Byrd, EVP & CFO, stated, "Objectively, we are the lowest cost provider. You can look at the data and the facts are there. Objectively, we have the best customer experience. We've won for the last 16 quarters with reputation.com comparing to Carvana, CarMax and others."

Outlook

  • The company reaffirmed its intention to expand EchoPark to reach 1 million vehicles sold annually and 90% market coverage in the U.S., beginning a new wave of store openings in late 2026 and accelerating in 2027.
  • Management guided to high single-digit volume growth for EchoPark in 2026, with expectations of accelerated growth in 2027 due to brand investments and additional store openings.
  • Frank Dyke, President & Director, projected new car GPUs in the franchise segment between $2,700 to $3,000 per unit, potentially stronger in the first quarter of 2026, and foresees affordability issues as new car prices continue to rise into the summer.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter adjusted EPS was $1.52 per share. Consolidated total revenues were $3.9 billion. Consolidated adjusted EBITDA was flat year-over-year for the quarter.
  • Franchise dealership reported revenues of $3.4 billion, with same-store new vehicle retail volume down 11% and used vehicle retail volume up 5% year-over-year. Franchised F&I gross profit per unit reached a record $2,624.
  • EchoPark segment recorded revenues of $481 million, down 5% year-over-year, but gross profit of $54 million was up 9% year-over-year. Segment retail unit sales volume decreased 6% year-over-year, while total GPU reached $3,420 per unit.
  • Powersports segment achieved revenues of $36 million, up 19% year-over-year, and gross profit of $9 million, up 25% year-over-year.
  • The company repurchased 600,000 shares in Q4 for $38 million.

Q&A

  • Jeffrey Lick, Stephens Inc.: Asked about EchoPark's competitive positioning. Frank Dyke responded that EchoPark is the "Costco sort of the pre-owned world," aiming for low prices and high guest satisfaction, planning to methodically grow the brand with 1–2 store openings in Q4 2026 and more in 2027.
  • John Babcock, Barclays: Asked about timing and focus of EchoPark advertising spend. Dyke said spending begins in the second quarter of 2026 with public campaigns launching in Q4, focusing on brand awareness, pricing, and experience.
  • Babcock also queried about new car GPU cadence. Dyke projected $2,700 to $3,000 per unit and warned of price inflation as OEMs push costs to consumers.
  • Rajat Gupta, JPMorgan: Asked if investments set up EchoPark for stronger growth in 2027. Dyke confirmed, "Yes. 100%." He also asked about fixed ops technician growth, with Dyke reporting an increase of 400 technicians since March 2024.
  • Bret Jordan, Jefferies: Inquired about the impact of increased advertising on 2027 results. Dyke confirmed accelerated volume and EBITDA growth expected in 2027 and beyond.
  • Christopher Pierce, Needham & Company: Asked about fixed ops subscription products and OEM support. Dyke noted current opportunities to expand, with marketing and pricing improvements planned. Byrd added that marketing for service is a new focus.
  • Michael Ward, Citi: Asked about EchoPark growth drivers. Byrd highlighted returning inventory and improved internal efficiencies. Ward confirmed the goal of 1 million units for EchoPark. Dyke provided updates on JLR inventory and BEV mix normalization.
  • Glenn Chin, Seaport: Asked about OEM margin cuts and decontenting. Dyke confirmed both are occurring as manufacturers address tariff pressures.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a slightly positive tone, recognizing record results and strategic clarity but pressing on margin pressures, OEM pass-through costs, and EchoPark ramp-up timing.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone during prepared remarks and the Q&A, emphasizing disciplined growth, cost leadership, and strategic investment. Phrases such as "We feel very comfortable" and "we are well positioned" were frequent. In Q&A, management addressed concerns directly without defensiveness.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts exhibited increased confidence, with more focus on EchoPark expansion and less concern about BEV inventory headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from cautious optimism in Q3 regarding BEV and inventory headwinds to strategic expansion and investment in EchoPark for Q4, with explicit targets for market coverage and volume.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward long-term EchoPark growth, marketing investment, and leveraging non-auction inventory sourcing.
  • Analysts moved from concerns about BEV impacts and inventory normalization in Q3 to questions about EchoPark's competitive positioning and growth acceleration in Q4.
  • Key metric changes include higher adjusted EPS and EBITDA growth for the full year, record gross profit in franchise and EchoPark segments, a reduction in BEV headwinds, and a significant increase in fixed ops technician headcount.
  • Management tone was more confident in Q4, with clearer articulation of long-term strategy and capability to absorb OEM cost increases.

Risks and Concerns

  • Ongoing headwinds from OEM tariff pass-throughs, margin cuts, and vehicle price inflation were highlighted by management and analysts.
  • Management is mitigating inventory sourcing risk by leveraging franchise dealerships for EchoPark supply and reducing auction dependence.
  • Affordability challenges for new vehicles are expected to intensify as OEMs increase prices, potentially impacting retail volume and mix.
  • Management cited potential variability in timing and returns from increased advertising spend for EchoPark, with benefits not expected until Q4 2026 or later.

Final Takeaway

Sonic Automotive’s fourth quarter call emphasized a disciplined, long-term strategy focused on expanding EchoPark to reach over 1 million annual unit sales and 90% market coverage, supported by a significant investment in brand marketing and a methodical approach to store openings. Management reported record profitability in high-margin business lines, a robust liquidity position, and a clear plan for leveraging internal inventory sources to support growth, while remaining vigilant about OEM pricing actions and external market pressures. The company’s outlook signals confidence in navigating industry headwinds and advancing its strategic priorities into 2026 and beyond.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: DoorDash outlines 2026 EBITDA margin increase and global tech stack rollout while expanding new verticals
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: DoorDash (DASH) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Tony Xu stated that DoorDash is "the leading player in many of the countries" in Europe and highlighted a strong start with the Deliveroo acquisition, reporting faster growth at the same profit contribution as previously expected and market share gains in key areas. Xu emphasized that "business outside of the U.S." is growing faster than within, while the U.S. itself had "2 of the fastest-growing quarters in 2025 in the last 4 years." Xu noted improvements to unit economics across the board.

  • CFO Ravi Inukonda said, "my expectation for the full year EBITDA for '26 has not changed since the last call. The way I think about it is 2026 EBITDA margin is going to be up slightly compared to 2025, excluding ROO and ROO to produce about $200 million of EBITDA, like we said. So that remains very consistent." Inukonda also explained that investments in 2026 will focus on the global tech stack, autonomy, and merchant services, with "the quantum of investment dollars... very similar to what I had expected at the time of the last call."

  • Xu described DoorDash's progress in expanding into categories outside restaurants, stating "around 30% of customers are ordering outside of the restaurant category" and highlighted growth in grocery and retail, where DoorDash claims to be "the leading third-party transaction platform in the U.S." and growing rapidly internationally.

Outlook

  • Inukonda maintained guidance for 2026 EBITDA margin to be "up slightly compared to 2025, excluding ROO and ROO to produce about $200 million of EBITDA."

  • Management stated that "majority of that [tech stack investment] should be in '26, some of that will be in '27, which will come off, but that's a smaller component of the overall spend." There was no change in full-year margin expectations from the previous quarter.

Financial Results

  • Xu and Inukonda highlighted that new verticals, including retail and grocery, had "a really strong quarter as well as the year," and cited that "30% of our MAUs in the U.S. order from categories outside of restaurants."

  • Inukonda indicated that profitability in the retail and grocery business is improving and expects "our entire retail and grocery business to be unit economic positive in the second half of the year."

  • Xu referenced continued high growth rates in grocery, stating "very high growth rates... in the grocery sector, not just in Q4, but also for this year as well."

Q&A

  • Shweta Khajuria, Wolfe Research: Asked about competitive intensity in Europe and investment cadence into 2027. Xu responded that DoorDash is gaining share in Europe's largest markets and is "growing much faster at the same profit contribution that we expected before the acquisition." Inukonda clarified that most tech stack investment is in 2026 with a smaller portion in 2027.

  • Michael Morton, MoffettNathanson: Inquired about investments for longer-distance deliveries and updates on DashMart and DashLink. Xu explained investments are enhancing Dasher pay models and app experience for more complex orders, while expanding fulfillment services with partners like Kroger and CVS.

  • Deepak Mathivanan, Cantor Fitzgerald: Asked about the autonomous delivery platform strategy. Xu said, "the autonomous delivery platform is probably the most valuable part of what we're building," with a focus in 2026 on orchestrating delivery use cases between Dashers and autonomous vehicles.

  • Youssef Squali, Truist Securities: Queried about Amazon's competition in grocery and Q1 margin headwinds. Xu noted no impact from Amazon and continued strong grocery growth; Inukonda cited seasonal effects and front-loaded ROO investment impacting Q1 EBITDA.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, pressing for details on investment cadence, competitive dynamics, and new business verticals but not expressing overt skepticism. Questions focused on growth opportunities, investment discipline, and tech stack execution.

  • Management maintained a confident and consistent tone, reinforcing guidance and strategic priorities. Xu stated, "we feel really great about our position in Europe," and Inukonda reiterated that "expectation for the full year EBITDA for '26 has not changed since the last call."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained similar tones, with management consistently emphasizing investment discipline and growth, and analysts seeking clarity on execution and future profitability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent, with no changes to 2026 EBITDA margin targets or investment levels.

  • Management focus continues to be on global tech stack integration, Deliveroo and Wolt integration, and expanding new verticals. The emphasis on the path to profitability for grocery and retail verticals is more pronounced.

  • Analysts' questions shifted modestly toward specifics on investment duration and realization of synergies from recent acquisitions.

  • Management's confidence in international growth and new verticals was reiterated, while the cadence of technology investment and synergy realization with Deliveroo was more detailed than in the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged redundant costs in running multiple tech stacks and the need for parallel investments during integration periods.

  • Seasonal impacts on margins and the front-loaded nature of investments, particularly in ROO, were discussed as near-term headwinds.

  • Xu described the complexity and uncertainty of integrating and expanding autonomous delivery, highlighting that "it's not going to be immediately... obvious to me that autonomous vehicles are going to perform every single type of delivery."

Final Takeaway

DoorDash's management reaffirmed its strategy of disciplined investment in technology, autonomy, and new business verticals, while maintaining consistent margin guidance and highlighting strong growth internationally and outside the core restaurant segment. The company expects the majority of its tech stack investment to occur in 2026, with profitability improvements anticipated for its grocery and retail businesses in the second half of the year. Management underscored confidence in its position in competitive markets, the successful integration of Deliveroo, and its ongoing commitment to expanding the operating system for local commerce.
